title: Control channel carrier switching for subslot-based cells
abstract: Certain aspects of the present disclosure provide techniques for facilitating control channel carrier switching for subslot-based cell. A method that may be performed by a user equipment (UE) includes receiving signaling configuring a first cell with physical uplink control channel (PUCCH) resources, configuring a second cell with PUCCH resources, and configuring at least one of the first cell or the second cell for subslot-based hybrid automatic repeat request acknowledgment (HARQ-ACK) reporting, receiving a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH), selecting, based on target PUCCH cell switching information, the first cell or the second cell as a target PUCCH cell for transmitting a PUCCH with HARQ-ACK feedback for the PDCCH or a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) scheduled by the PDCCH, and transmitting the PUCCH with the HARQ-ACK feedback in a subslot or slot on the target PUCCH cell, in accordance with the selection.
claims: 1. A method for wireless communication by a user equipment (UE), comprising: receiving signaling configuring a first cell with physical uplink control channel (PUCCH) resources, configuring a second cell with PUCCH resources, and configuring at least one of the first cell or the second cell for subslot-based hybrid automatic repeat request acknowledgment (HARQ-ACK) reporting; receiving a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH); selecting, based on target PUCCH cell switching information, the first cell or the second cell as a target PUCCH cell for transmitting a PUCCH with HARQ-ACK feedback for the PDCCH or a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) scheduled by the PDCCH; and transmitting the PUCCH with the HARQ-ACK feedback in a subslot or slot on the target PUCCH cell, in accordance with the selection.
